title: Result - Slovakia
content:
Welcome to Slovakia, a Central European gem with 40% of its territory covered in lush forests. Bordered by 5 countries and boasting ports on the Danube at Bratislava and Komarno, Slovakia blends rich folk traditions with influences from Austrian, German, Polish and Hungarian cultures.
Explore Slovakia's stunning natural beauty, from rocky mountain ranges and deep gorges to serene valleys and waterfalls. Discover rare flora and fauna, hike scenic trails and unwind in some of Central Europe’s oldest and finest spas. Slovakia promises a captivating mix of nature and culture.

## Unique food tradition
Bryndzové halušky (potato dumpling): small and tender dumplings tossed in a creamy sheep's milk cheese sauce and topped with salty bacon.
The country prides itself on its industrial heritage which has provided a stable base for the development of certain sectors such as automotive and electronics.

### Local legend or myth
Juraj Jánošík: known as the Slovak Robin Hood, is celebrated for stealing from the rich to aid the poor. Born in Terchová, Northern Slovakia, he is a symbol of bravery and resistance.

### Language of the Land
Slovak is a West Slavic language that's closely related to Czech, Polish and the languages of the former Yugoslavia.
caves are found in Slovakia, home to many UNESCO-listed sites like Domica Cave and Slovak Karst.
